fields.name: official project name
fields.features: 8 differentiating features, ranked by implementation priority (index 0 = build first)
fields.impact: social, economic, environmental, technological (with quantified metrics where feasible)

projects.name: FungalForecast
projects.shortDescription: Smallholder farmers lose 20-40% of staple crops to fungal disease detected too late for treatment. FungalForecast turns a single phone photo of a leaf, combined with hyperlocal humidity and temperature data, into a same-day disease-risk forecast and a costed treatment plan in the farmer's own language. The target market is the ~500M smallholder farms in South Asia, Sub-Saharan Africa, and Southeast Asia.
features: On-device leaf-photo classifier that works offline and returns a result in under 3 seconds on a $60 Android phone
Hyperlocal 5-day infection-risk forecast fusing the photo diagnosis with gridded weather and microclimate data
Costed, locally-sourced treatment recommendations (organic and chemical) with dosage by field size
Voice-first, low-literacy interface in regional languages with audio explanations
Community outbreak map so neighboring farmers get pre-emptive alerts before symptoms appear
Agro-dealer integration showing which recommended inputs are in stock nearby and at what price
Confidence scoring that routes uncertain cases to a human agronomist queue
Yield-loss-averted ledger that quantifies value for microfinance and crop-insurance partners

operational: Curated regional disease image dataset per crop
Agronomist review panel for low-confidence cases
regulatory: National pesticide registration and label compliance
Data protection for farmer location and yield data

impact.social: Reduces dependence on misinformed input use; empowers low-literacy farmers with expert-grade guidance reaching potentially tens of millions of growers.
impact.economic: Averting even 10% of fungal yield loss on a 1-hectare rice plot can preserve $150-300 of annual income per farmer.
impact.environmental: Targeted treatment cuts blanket pesticide spraying by an estimated 25-40%, reducing soil and waterway contamination.
impact.technological: Demonstrates robust edge-AI inference under intermittent connectivity and pushes federated model improvement from field data.

projects.name: ConceptGap
projects.shortDescription: Standard quizzes tell a student they got a question wrong but not why, leaving misconceptions to compound silently. ConceptGap is a diagnostic engine that maps each learner's misconception graph across a subject and generates targeted micro-remediation, rather than just scoring right/wrong. The target market is secondary and tertiary STEM educators and self-directed learners.
features: Misconception-tagged item bank where each wrong answer is mapped to a specific underlying error
Knowledge-graph model that pinpoints the root concept causing a cluster of mistakes
Adaptive micro-remediation that targets the root gap rather than re-teaching the whole topic
Teacher dashboard showing class-wide misconception heatmaps to guide live instruction
Spaced-retrieval scheduler that re-tests resolved gaps to confirm durable learning
Open authoring tools so educators can add misconception-tagged items for any curriculum
Explainable feedback that names the specific reasoning error in plain language
Curriculum-alignment mapping to national standards for adoption by schools

operational: Continuous item validation and psychometric review

impact.social: Shifts assessment from ranking to genuine understanding, narrowing achievement gaps for struggling learners.
impact.economic: Reduces remedial-course costs and teacher time spent re-diagnosing; targeted practice can cut time-to-mastery by 20-30%.
impact.environmental: Largely software-based with negligible direct footprint; reduces printed assessment materials.
impact.technological: Advances interpretable learner modeling and knowledge-graph-based adaptive learning.

projects.name: SplitVest
projects.shortDescription: Hundreds of millions of people save through informal rotating savings groups (ROSCAs, susu, chit funds) that rely on cash and trust, exposing members to fraud, disputes, and zero credit history. SplitVest digitizes these groups with a transparent shared ledger, automated payout scheduling, and an optional bridge to formal savings products. The target market is unbanked and underbanked communities across emerging markets.
features: Transparent shared ledger every member can audit, replacing the cash-box treasurer model
Automated contribution reminders and payout-order scheduling with dispute logging
Reputation and reliability scoring built from on-time contributions, exportable as alternative credit data
Flexible group rules engine supporting regional ROSCA variants and custom payout logic
Optional escrow and group insurance fund to cover member defaults
Offline-capable, low-data interface with SMS fallback for feature phones
Bridge to regulated savings/credit products once a group builds a track record
Multi-signature payout approvals to prevent treasurer fraud

impact.social: Brings transparency and dispute reduction to a deeply trusted but fragile financial practice for the unbanked.
impact.economic: Builds alternative credit histories that can unlock formal loans; protects pooled savings that often represent months of household income.
impact.environmental: Reduces cash handling and travel for in-person group meetings.
impact.technological: Demonstrates trust-minimized shared ledgers and alternative-data credit scoring at the base of the pyramid.

projects.name: ClauseLens
projects.shortDescription: Most tenants sign leases they cannot fully understand and unknowingly accept unenforceable or predatory clauses. ClauseLens analyzes a lease in plain language, flags risky and jurisdiction-illegal terms, and suggests concrete negotiation points before signing. The target market is renters, tenant-rights organizations, and small landlords seeking compliant templates.
features: Clause-level risk flagging mapped to the specific local/state tenancy law that governs it
Plain-language rewrite of each dense clause with a clarity score
Jurisdiction-aware illegality detector for clauses void under local law
Negotiation playbook suggesting alternative wording the tenant can request
Side-by-side comparison against a fair-market baseline lease for the region
Tenant-rights resource routing to local legal aid when issues are serious
Landlord mode that generates compliant, fair lease templates
Document redaction and privacy-preserving processing of personal details

operational: Jurisdiction-specific legal rule libraries kept current

impact.social: Rebalances information asymmetry between landlords and renters, reducing exploitative housing arrangements.
impact.economic: Helps tenants avoid unjust fees and deposits potentially worth hundreds to thousands per lease.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint; reduces paper-based legal consultation.
impact.technological: Advances jurisdiction-aware legal NLP and explainable document analysis.

projects.name: ScamSentinel
projects.shortDescription: Phone and message scams steal billions annually, disproportionately targeting elderly and vulnerable people who can't always recognize manipulation in real time. ScamSentinel is an on-device assistant that detects scam patterns during live calls and messages and gently intervenes before money is lost. The target market is older adults, their family caregivers, and the telecom/bank ecosystem.
features: On-device speech and text analysis that flags known scam scripts in real time without sending audio to the cloud
Gentle, escalating interventions (warning tone, on-screen prompt) rather than abrupt call blocking
Caregiver alert option (opt-in) when a high-risk interaction is detected
Scam-pattern library continuously updated from reported emerging scams
Bank-handoff integration to pause or verify suspicious transactions mid-scam
Privacy-first design with no raw audio leaving the device
Plain-language education that builds the user's own scam-spotting skill over time

operational: On-device model deployment across phone hardware
Scam intelligence feed and rapid updates
regulatory: Data privacy and biometric voice rules

impact.social: Protects dignity and independence of older adults while reducing the trauma of being defrauded.
impact.economic: Preventing even a fraction of the billions lost to elder fraud yields large aggregate savings.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances privacy-preserving on-device NLP and real-time manipulation detection.

projects.name: HarvestLink
projects.shortDescription: Up to a third of produce is lost between farm and market while institutions overpay middlemen for the same food. HarvestLink is a direct procurement platform connecting farmer cooperatives to schools, hospitals, and canteens with demand forecasting and pooled logistics. The target market is institutional food buyers and smallholder cooperatives in emerging and developing economies.
features: Aggregated demand forecasting so co-ops plant and harvest to confirmed institutional orders
Pooled cold and dry logistics that small farmers can't afford individually
Transparent pricing that removes opaque middleman margins
Quality grading and traceability from farm to delivery
Pre-harvest contracts and partial advances to de-risk farmer income
Surplus-redirection to food banks to cut waste from overproduction
Mobile-first co-op management for low-connectivity rural settings
Impact dashboard quantifying loss avoided and farmer income gained

impact.social: Raises and stabilizes smallholder incomes while improving institutional food quality.
impact.economic: Cutting post-harvest loss and middleman margins can raise farmer revenue 15-30% and lower buyer costs.
impact.environmental: Reducing food loss avoids the wasted water, land, and emissions embedded in spoiled produce.
impact.technological: Advances demand forecasting and logistics pooling for fragmented agricultural supply chains.

projects.name: RehabRep
projects.shortDescription: Most physiotherapy happens unsupervised at home, where poor form and low adherence stall recovery and waste clinical effort. RehabRep uses a phone camera to coach exercise form in real time, track adherence, and feed progress back to the clinician. The target market is physiotherapy clinics, orthopedic and post-surgical patients, and remote-care providers.
features: Real-time form correction via on-device pose estimation with instant audio cues
Clinician-prescribed exercise programs with rep/set/range targets
Adherence and progress tracking that flags patients falling behind
Pain and difficulty logging that adapts program intensity safely
Clinician dashboard for remote review and program adjustment
Privacy-preserving processing that keeps video on-device
Gamified streaks and milestones to sustain motivation
Outcome analytics linking adherence and form quality to recovery speed

impact.social: Extends quality rehab to people who can't attend frequent in-person sessions, improving recovery equity.
impact.economic: Better adherence shortens recovery and reduces re-injury and repeat clinical visits.
impact.environmental: Reduces travel for routine rehab appointments.
impact.technological: Advances on-device biomechanical analysis and remote therapeutic monitoring.

projects.name: SkillBridge
projects.shortDescription: Graduates and job-seekers acquire credentials misaligned with what local employers actually need, fueling underemployment. SkillBridge is a micro-credential marketplace that maps real-time local employer demand to short, verifiable learning paths and connects completers directly to hiring partners. The target market is workforce-development agencies, employers, and adult learners in transitioning economies.
features: Live local labor-demand signal scraped and verified from employer hiring data
Demand-mapped learning paths bundling short courses into job-ready competencies
Verifiable, portable micro-credentials with tamper-evident issuance
Direct employer hiring pipeline for credential completers
Skills-gap diagnostics that recommend the highest-ROI next credential
Employer co-design tools so companies define and endorse credentials
Outcome tracking on placement rates and wage gains per credential
Subsidy and scholarship matching for learners

impact.social: Reduces underemployment and connects marginalized workers to genuine opportunity.
impact.economic: Aligning training to demand raises placement rates and wages; reduces wasted education spend.
impact.environmental: Largely digital; reduces commuting for traditional retraining.
impact.technological: Advances labor-market intelligence and verifiable credentialing.

projects.name: SoilBiome
projects.shortDescription: Farmers over-apply synthetic fertilizer partly because they have no view into their soil's living microbiome, degrading soil and waterways. SoilBiome offers affordable soil microbiome testing plus tailored biological amendment recommendations that cut chemical inputs while maintaining yield. The target market is mid-size farms, agronomists, and regenerative-agriculture programs.
features: Affordable mail-in soil microbiome sequencing with simplified sampling kits
Microbiome health scoring benchmarked against high-yield comparable soils
Tailored bio-fertilizer and amendment recommendations to rebalance the soil
Synthetic-input reduction plan that protects yield while cutting cost
Season-over-season tracking to show soil recovery trends
Agronomist collaboration tools for interpreting results
Carbon and soil-health credit documentation for emerging markets
Regional microbiome benchmark database that improves with scale

impact.social: Equips farmers with science previously available only to large agribusiness.
impact.economic: Reducing fertilizer use 20-30% while holding yield directly improves farm margins.
impact.environmental: Cuts nutrient runoff and emissions from synthetic fertilizer and supports soil carbon sequestration.
impact.technological: Advances affordable metagenomics and microbiome-to-recommendation modeling.

projects.name: GigShield
projects.shortDescription: Gig and informal workers face volatile income and no benefits safety net, leaving them one bad month from crisis. GigShield smooths income across gigs and pools portable benefits—savings, insurance, and emergency credit—funded by small automatic contributions. The target market is gig-economy and informal-sector workers and the platforms that engage them.
features: Income-smoothing buffer that evens out volatile weekly earnings
Portable benefits wallet (health, accident, retirement) that follows the worker across platforms
Automatic micro-contributions calibrated to each worker's cash flow
Emergency credit line priced on verified gig income history
Multi-platform earnings aggregation for a complete income picture
Benefit-matching with employer or platform contributions where available
Financial-health coaching tailored to irregular income

impact.social: Provides dignity and stability to a fast-growing, under-protected workforce.
impact.economic: Reduces costly emergency borrowing and income shocks; builds long-term financial resilience.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances alternative-income underwriting and portable-benefits infrastructure.

projects.name: NeighborCart
projects.shortDescription: Households overpay for groceries because they buy at retail unit prices while wholesale tiers stay out of reach. NeighborCart pools nearby neighbors into a single recurring bulk order that unlocks wholesale pricing, with one coordinator pickup and automatic per-member splitting. Target market: cost-conscious urban and suburban households and apartment communities.
features: One-tap join to a local pool tied to a recurring weekly cart
Automatic price-break engine that surfaces savings as the pool grows
Per-member cost splitting and in-app settlement, no cash handling
Coordinator rotation and single-drop pickup logistics
Wholesaler sourcing dashboard with minimum-order tracking
Shelf-life-aware basket suggestions to cut spoilage
Trust and reliability scoring for pool members
Community demand aggregation that lets new wholesalers bid for routes

impact.social: Builds neighbor cooperation and lowers food costs for budget-strained households.
impact.economic: Wholesale tiers commonly cut grocery costs 15-30% versus retail unit prices.
impact.environmental: Consolidated bulk delivery cuts last-mile trips and packaging per household.
impact.technological: Advances demand-pooling logistics and trust-based group commerce.

projects.name: TableTurn
projects.shortDescription: Restaurants lose revenue to empty covers and last-minute cancellations they can't refill fast enough. TableTurn detects soon-to-be-empty tables and pushes time-boxed, dynamically-priced offers to nearby diners to fill them in real time. Target market: independent and small-chain restaurants in dense areas.
features: Real-time empty-cover detection from POS and reservation feeds
Dynamic last-minute pricing tuned to fill within a set window
Geo-targeted offer push to nearby opted-in diners
No-show prediction that pre-lists at-risk tables
Margin guardrails so discounts never sell below floor
Diner taste profiles to match offers to likely takers
Performance analytics on covers recovered and revenue lifted

impact.social: Helps small restaurants survive thin margins and rewards spontaneous diners.
impact.economic: Filling even 2-3 otherwise-empty covers nightly can lift weekly revenue several percent.
impact.environmental: Reduces prepped-food waste from unsold capacity.
impact.technological: Advances real-time yield management for perishable service capacity.

projects.name: PantryRescue
projects.shortDescription: Cafes, bakeries, and grocers discard large volumes of perfectly edible end-of-day food while price-sensitive shoppers go without. PantryRescue is a marketplace selling surplus food as discounted surprise bundles for same-day pickup. Target market: food retailers with daily surplus and budget-conscious urban consumers.
features: Surprise-bundle listings posted in minutes near closing time
Time-windowed pickup slots to clear stock before disposal
Dynamic discounting as the pickup window shrinks
Merchant waste analytics to right-size future production
Allergen and dietary tagging on bundles
Impact tracker quantifying meals saved per merchant
Route-friendly map clustering for multi-stop pickups

impact.social: Improves affordable food access while normalizing waste reduction.
impact.economic: Recovers revenue on food otherwise written off; shoppers save 50-70% per bundle.
impact.environmental: Diverting edible food from landfill avoids significant methane and embedded resources.
impact.technological: Advances perishable-inventory liquidation and waste analytics.

projects.name: TutorTrail
projects.shortDescription: Parents pay for tutoring with no reliable signal of whether it actually improves outcomes. TutorTrail is an outcome-based tutoring marketplace that matches students to tutors, tracks measured progress, and ties tutor ranking to verified results. Target market: families seeking accountable academic support and qualified tutors.
features: Outcome tracking via standardized pre/post assessments
Tutor ranking driven by verified student gains, not just reviews
Smart matching on subject, level, and learning style
Session notes and progress timeline shared with parents
Curriculum alignment to local exam boards
Group-session formation to lower cost per student

impact.social: Brings accountability and access to private tutoring across income levels.
impact.economic: Outcome transparency reduces wasted tutoring spend and rewards effective tutors.
impact.environmental: Online-first model reduces commuting to lessons.
impact.technological: Advances verifiable learning-outcome measurement in a marketplace.

projects.name: FixFleet
projects.shortDescription: Home appliance repair is slow and unreliable because technicians arrive without the right parts. FixFleet diagnoses the likely fault before dispatch and pre-stages the needed parts so most jobs are fixed on the first visit. Target market: homeowners, property managers, and independent repair technicians.
features: Pre-visit fault diagnosis from symptoms, model, and photos
Parts pre-staging so technicians arrive equipped
Warranty and repair-history record per appliance
Inventory forecasting for common failure parts
Repair-vs-replace guidance to avoid needless purchases

impact.social: Reduces household disruption and extends appliance lifespans.
impact.economic: First-time-fix rates can rise from ~70% toward 90%+, cutting repeat-visit costs.
impact.environmental: Repair-over-replace and fewer truck rolls cut waste and emissions.
impact.technological: Advances pre-dispatch diagnostics and parts-demand forecasting.

projects.name: CraftLedger
projects.shortDescription: Artisans and small makers chronically underprice their work and lose track of true costs, eroding already-thin livelihoods. CraftLedger is a pricing and bookkeeping copilot that computes real per-item cost, suggests profitable pricing, and keeps simple books. Target market: independent makers, craftspeople, and micro-manufacturers.
features: True-cost calculator including materials, time, and overhead
Profitable price suggestions benchmarked to comparable products
Marketplace fee and shipping cost modeling
Cash-flow view for irregular maker income

impact.social: Sustains creative livelihoods often undervalued by their own makers.
impact.economic: Correct pricing can lift maker margins by double-digit percentages.
impact.environmental: Better material tracking reduces over-purchasing and waste.
impact.technological: Advances cost-aware pricing intelligence for micro-businesses.

projects.name: ResaleRoute
projects.shortDescription: Small boutiques want to capture the booming resale market but lack logistics for authentication, listing, and consignment payouts. ResaleRoute is a managed resale backend that handles intake, authentication, listing, and consignor payments for independent retailers. Target market: independent fashion and goods boutiques and their consignors.
features: Standardized intake and condition grading workflow
Auto-listing across the boutique's storefront and channels
Consignor payout ledger with transparent splits
Dynamic markdown scheduling to clear aging stock
Returns and dispute handling for secondhand goods
Sustainability reporting on items diverted from disposal

impact.social: Extends product life and gives small retailers a resale revenue stream.
impact.economic: Opens a high-growth resale channel without large operational overhead.
impact.environmental: Each resold garment offsets the footprint of a new purchase.
impact.technological: Advances managed consignment operations and provenance tracking.

projects.name: RentRoll
projects.shortDescription: Small landlords with a handful of units juggle rent collection, maintenance, and compliance with spreadsheets and texts, leading to missed payments and disputes. RentRoll automates rent collection, maintenance dispatch, and record-keeping for non-professional landlords. Target market: independent landlords owning 1-20 units.
features: Automated rent collection with reminders and late tracking
Tenant maintenance requests routed to vetted contractors
Digital lease storage and renewal alerts
Compliance checklist for local rental laws
Expense and income tracking for tax season
Tenant communication log for dispute protection

impact.social: Improves landlord-tenant clarity and reduces avoidable disputes.
impact.economic: Automated collection cuts late and missed rent; saves hours of admin per unit monthly.
impact.environmental: Digitizes paper-heavy rental administration.
impact.technological: Advances accessible property operations for non-professional owners.

projects.name: PetPath
projects.shortDescription: Pet owners react to illness instead of preventing it, driving costly emergencies and poorer outcomes. PetPath is a preventive pet-health subscription pairing scheduled care reminders with vet telehealth and a longitudinal health record. Target market: dog and cat owners and small veterinary practices.
features: Preventive care calendar tailored to breed, age, and risk
Longitudinal health record portable across clinics

impact.social: Improves animal welfare and reduces owner anxiety and emergency stress.
impact.economic: Preventive care lowers lifetime pet-health costs versus emergency-driven spending.
impact.environmental: Reduces unnecessary clinic trips via telehealth triage.
impact.technological: Advances longitudinal veterinary records and remote triage.

projects.name: SecondStitch
projects.shortDescription: Consumers discard clothing that could be repaired or shared, fueling textile waste and waste of money. SecondStitch is a D2C service combining doorstep garment repair with a rental option for occasional-wear items to extend each garment's useful life. Target market: sustainability-minded urban consumers and apparel brands seeking circular services.
features: Doorstep pickup-and-return repair with transparent pricing
Rental option for occasion and seasonal wear
Garment care passport tracking repairs and wear
Brand partnerships for white-label circular services
Carbon-saved tracker per repair or rental
Subscription tier for unlimited minor repairs

impact.social: Revives repair culture and creates work for skilled menders.
impact.economic: Extends garment life, reducing consumer replacement spend.
impact.environmental: Each extended garment lifecycle avoids new-production footprint and textile landfill.
impact.technological: Advances circular-service logistics and garment lifecycle tracking.

projects.name: MealMap
projects.shortDescription: Patients with chronic conditions struggle to follow medical diets, and payers see avoidable readmissions as a result. MealMap delivers medically-tailored meal plans designed with clinicians and bills eligible costs to insurers. Target market: chronic-condition patients, payers, and health systems.
features: Clinician-designed meal plans per condition (diabetes, CKD, CHF)
Outcome reporting tied to readmission reduction
Step-down from delivered meals to self-cooking guidance

impact.social: Improves disease management and quality of life for vulnerable patients.
impact.economic: Medically-tailored meals have demonstrated meaningful reductions in costly readmissions.
impact.environmental: Local sourcing and portioning reduce food waste.
impact.technological: Advances food-as-medicine programs and adherence analytics.

projects.name: FarmStand Direct
projects.shortDescription: Small farms want subscription (CSA) revenue but can't manage box logistics, sign-ups, and flexible substitutions at scale. FarmStand Direct runs the operational stack for farm subscription boxes, from member management to route-optimized delivery. Target market: small and mid-size farms and local subscribers.
features: Route-optimized local delivery and pickup hubs
Surplus and shortage balancing across the season

impact.social: Strengthens local food systems and direct farmer-consumer ties.
impact.economic: Subscription revenue stabilizes farmer cash flow against market swings.
impact.environmental: Short local supply chains cut food miles and packaging.
impact.technological: Advances harvest-driven logistics and demand forecasting for small farms.

projects.name: EventEcho
projects.shortDescription: Local event organizers patch together ticketing, promotion, and audience data across disconnected tools, losing repeat attendees. EventEcho unifies micro-event ticketing with an audience CRM so organizers build and re-engage their own community. Target market: independent event organizers, venues, and community groups.
features: Fast ticketing with low flat fees for small events
Owned audience CRM that survives across events

impact.social: Helps grassroots culture and community events sustain themselves.
impact.economic: Lower fees and owned audiences raise organizer margins and repeat attendance.
impact.environmental: Digital ticketing reduces paper and travel for box-office tasks.
impact.technological: Advances integrated commerce-plus-CRM for the long tail of events.

projects.name: LoyaltyLoop
projects.shortDescription: Independent merchants can't compete with chains' loyalty programs and lose repeat customers as a result. LoyaltyLoop is a shared loyalty network where small local businesses pool rewards, so points earned at one shop are spendable across the network. Target market: independent retailers, cafes, and service businesses in a local district.
features: Cross-merchant shared points usable across the network
Zero-hardware enrollment via phone number or QR
District-level campaigns that drive foot traffic between members
Settlement engine balancing redemptions across members
Customer insights without sharing raw personal data

impact.social: Strengthens local high streets against chain dominance.
impact.economic: Shared loyalty lifts repeat visits and cross-shop spending for small businesses.
impact.environmental: Promotes local shopping with shorter trips.
impact.technological: Advances multi-party loyalty settlement and privacy-preserving insights.

projects.name: VowVault
projects.shortDescription: Couples coordinate dozens of wedding vendors over scattered chats and risky cash deposits, with little protection if a vendor fails. VowVault centralizes vendor booking, timelines, and milestone-based escrow payments for events. Target market: engaged couples, event planners, and wedding vendors.
features: Vendor marketplace with verified reviews and availability
Shared event timeline syncing all vendors

impact.social: Reduces stress and financial risk around major life events.
impact.economic: Escrow protection prevents lost deposits and reduces vendor no-shows.
impact.environmental: Digital coordination cuts paper and redundant travel.
impact.technological: Advances milestone escrow and multi-vendor coordination.

projects.name: CommuteClub
projects.shortDescription: Suburban commuters drive alone at high cost while employers chase sustainability goals they can't operationalize. CommuteClub is an employer-sponsored vanpool and carpool marketplace that matches colleagues into shared commutes with subsidized seats. Target market: employers with commuting workforces and their employees.
features: Colleague matching by route, schedule, and preference
Dynamic vanpool formation and seat booking
Commute emissions and savings dashboard for HR
Integration with transit for first/last mile

impact.social: Eases commute stress and builds workplace community.
impact.economic: Shared commutes cut individual transport costs and employer parking demand.
impact.environmental: Each filled vanpool seat removes a solo car trip, cutting commute emissions materially.
impact.technological: Advances dynamic ride-matching for recurring commutes.

projects.name: GymGrid
projects.shortDescription: Gyms and studios run far below capacity at off-peak hours while price-sensitive members can't find affordable access. GymGrid is a marketplace selling discounted off-peak class and facility capacity, filling empty slots without cannibalizing prime memberships. Target market: independent gyms, studios, and flexible fitness consumers.
features: Member-overlap rules to avoid revenue cannibalization
Demand analytics to guide off-peak programming

impact.social: Expands affordable fitness access and smooths demand.
impact.economic: Monetizes idle capacity that is otherwise pure loss for operators.
impact.environmental: Higher utilization improves energy efficiency per visit.
impact.technological: Advances perishable-capacity yield management for fitness.

projects.name: ShelfShare
projects.shortDescription: Independent bookstores carry rich local inventory that is invisible online, sending readers to large platforms by default. ShelfShare gives indie bookstores a unified online storefront with real-time local inventory and same-day pickup or local delivery. Target market: independent bookstores and local readers.
features: Real-time local inventory synced from store systems
Unified storefront across participating indie stores
Same-day pickup and local delivery options
Fair revenue split favoring the local store
Demand signals to guide local buying

impact.social: Keeps cultural anchors and local curation alive against platform dominance.
impact.economic: Captures online demand for local stores that currently leaks to big platforms.
impact.environmental: Local fulfillment shortens delivery distances.
impact.technological: Advances federated local-inventory commerce.

projects.name: DentDesk
projects.shortDescription: Dental practices lose 10-20% of revenue to no-shows and idle chair time while patients delay care over cost uncertainty. DentDesk cuts no-shows with smart recall and waitlist fill, and offers transparent treatment estimates with at-chair financing. Target market: independent dental practices and their patients.
features: Smart recall scheduling tied to each treatment interval
No-show prediction with automated waitlist backfill
Two-way patient reminders across SMS and app

impact.social: Improves access to dental care patients otherwise postpone.
impact.economic: Recovering no-show and idle-chair revenue can lift practice income 8-15%.
impact.environmental: Digital recalls replace paper mailers.
impact.technological: Advances no-show prediction and chair-yield optimization.

projects.name: ClinicFlow
projects.shortDescription: Outpatient clinics in dense and emerging-market cities run on chaotic first-come queues, costing patients whole days and clinics throughput. ClinicFlow gives token-based appointments, live queue position, and flow analytics so patients arrive at the right time. Target market: outpatient clinics, diagnostic centers, and their patients.
features: Token-based appointment slots replacing physical queues
Live queue position and wait-time estimates
SMS/app notifications to time patient arrival
Doctor and room flow analytics to cut bottlenecks

impact.social: Saves patients hours and reduces crowding and infection risk in waiting rooms.
impact.economic: Higher throughput lets clinics see more patients without added capacity.
impact.environmental: Fewer wasted trips and shorter idle time on-site.
impact.technological: Advances healthcare flow optimization for low-resource settings.

projects.name: SaverStreak
projects.shortDescription: Young adults struggle to build savings habits when saving feels joyless and abstract. SaverStreak turns micro-saving into a streak-and-goal game that automatically rounds up and sets aside small amounts toward real goals. Target market: young adults and first-time savers in cash-and-mobile economies.
features: Streaks and milestones that reward consistency
Visual goal jars for concrete targets
Withdrawal friction tuned to protect goals
Yield routing to regulated savings products
Spending insight nudges tied to goals

impact.social: Builds durable financial habits for people who never learned to save.
impact.economic: Even small automated savings compound into meaningful buffers against shocks.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances behavioral fintech and safe-to-save modeling.

projects.name: HomePlate
projects.shortDescription: Talented home cooks can't legally sell their food, and neighbors miss affordable authentic meals. HomePlate is a licensed home-kitchen food marketplace that handles food-safety compliance, ordering, and local pickup or delivery. Target market: home-based cooks and local food buyers in jurisdictions with cottage-food or microenterprise laws.
features: Cook storefronts with menus and capacity limits
Local ordering with pickup and short-radius delivery
Payouts with tax-ready records for cooks

impact.social: Creates income for home cooks and preserves local food culture.
impact.economic: Opens a compliant micro-enterprise income stream with low startup cost.
impact.environmental: Short-radius local fulfillment cuts delivery distance.
impact.technological: Advances compliance-gated local food commerce.

projects.name: MendMeet
projects.shortDescription: People throw away repairable goods because repair skills, tools, and trustworthy fixers are hard to find locally. MendMeet organizes community repair events and a shared tool library, matching broken items to volunteer and pro fixers. Target market: sustainability-minded communities, councils, and repair-skilled volunteers.
features: Fixer matching by item type and skill
Council and sponsor reporting on waste diverted

impact.social: Builds community skills and reduces throwaway culture.
impact.economic: Saves households repair-vs-replace costs and builds local skills.
impact.environmental: Each repaired item avoids new-production footprint and landfill.
impact.technological: Advances community circular-economy coordination.

projects.name: LangLoop
projects.shortDescription: Language learners plateau because apps lack real conversation and tutors are costly to schedule. LangLoop matches learners into short, structured conversation micro-sessions with peers and affordable native speakers, with AI feedback between sessions. Target market: intermediate language learners and part-time native-speaker coaches.
features: Short structured conversation sessions matched by level
AI feedback on fluency and errors between sessions
Topic and scenario packs for practical use

impact.social: Democratizes conversation practice that drives real fluency.
impact.economic: Micro-sessions cost a fraction of traditional tutoring and create flexible coach income.
impact.environmental: Fully remote; no commuting.
impact.technological: Advances speech feedback and conversation matching.

projects.name: LeafLogic
projects.shortDescription: Urban gardeners kill plants through guesswork on watering, light, and disease, abandoning the hobby. LeafLogic diagnoses plant problems from photos and delivers a personalized care schedule plus a subscription for the right inputs. Target market: home plant owners and urban gardening retailers.
features: Personalized watering and light schedules by species and home conditions
Pest and disease identification with treatment steps
Care reminders tuned to season and location
Input subscription (soil, nutrients) matched to needs

impact.social: Sustains a wellbeing-boosting hobby and greens urban homes.
impact.economic: Reduces wasted plant and input spend and builds retailer recurring revenue.
impact.environmental: Fewer dead plants and right-sized inputs reduce waste.
impact.technological: Advances consumer plant diagnostics and personalized care.

projects.name: SafeStep
projects.shortDescription: Solo travelers and night commuters face safety risk with no reliable check-in or vetted local help. SafeStep provides scheduled safety check-ins, trusted-contact escalation, and a network of verified local guides and safe stops. Target market: solo travelers, women commuting at night, and tourism operators.
features: Scheduled check-ins with auto-escalation if missed
One-tap alert to trusted contacts with live location
Verified local guide and safe-stop network
Route safety scoring from community and incident data

impact.social: Increases personal safety and confidence for vulnerable travelers.
impact.economic: Supports tourism and creates verified-guide income.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances safety check-in and trust-network systems.

projects.name: SplitNest
projects.shortDescription: Roommates fight over shared bills, groceries, and chores tracked in messy chats and spreadsheets. SplitNest auto-reconciles shared household expenses, splits fairly, and settles balances, with optional chore rotation. Target market: shared-housing renters and student households.
features: Automatic shared-expense capture and fair splitting
Spending transparency without exposing private accounts

impact.social: Reduces household conflict and improves shared-living harmony.
impact.economic: Prevents unpaid shared costs and uneven burden.
impact.environmental: Digital; reduces paper tracking.
impact.technological: Advances automated multi-party expense reconciliation.

projects.name: CareCircle
projects.shortDescription: Families caring for aging parents juggle appointments, medications, and updates across siblings with no shared system, causing errors and burnout. CareCircle coordinates a family's caregiving with shared schedules, medication tracking, and provider updates. Target market: family caregivers of elderly or chronically ill relatives.
features: Shared care calendar across family members
Provider visit notes and document vault
Alerts for missed meds or appointments

impact.social: Reduces caregiver burnout and care-coordination errors for vulnerable elders.
impact.economic: Prevents costly medication errors and missed-care complications.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances family care-coordination and adherence tracking.

projects.name: TradeProof
projects.shortDescription: Skilled trades workers without formal credentials struggle to prove competence to employers and clients. TradeProof builds a verified portfolio of completed work and short practical assessments that employers trust. Target market: tradespeople, contractors, and trade employers.
features: Verified work portfolio with client confirmation
Short practical skill assessments per trade

impact.social: Opens opportunity for skilled workers lacking formal paper credentials.
impact.economic: Faster trusted hiring and better wage matching for verified skills.
impact.environmental: Digital portfolios reduce paper certificates.
impact.technological: Advances practical competency verification and portable credentials.

projects.name: GiveRoute
projects.shortDescription: Usable goods go to landfill because donating them is inconvenient and charities can't coordinate pickups efficiently. GiveRoute matches households' donatable items to charities that want them and optimizes pickup routes. Target market: charities, thrift networks, and decluttering households.
features: Item-to-charity matching by what each org actually needs
Photo-based item listing and condition tagging

impact.social: Channels usable goods to people who need them and supports charities.
impact.economic: Cuts charity logistics cost and gives donors easy tax benefit.
impact.environmental: Diverts reusable goods from landfill at scale.
impact.technological: Advances need-matching and donation logistics optimization.

projects.name: FreelanceFort
projects.shortDescription: Freelancers routinely get stiffed on payment and lack the leverage and paperwork to protect themselves. FreelanceFort provides milestone contracts, escrowed payments, and late-payment enforcement tools so independents get paid. Target market: freelancers, independent contractors, and their clients.
features: Escrowed client payments released on delivery
Optional income advance against approved milestones

impact.social: Protects independent workers' livelihoods and reduces payment stress.
impact.economic: Reduces unpaid and late invoices that plague freelancers.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances milestone escrow and freelance payment protection.

projects.name: AllergyAtlas
projects.shortDescription: People with food allergies risk their health every time they eat out because menus rarely disclose reliable allergen detail. AllergyAtlas maps allergen-safe dishes across restaurants, filtered to each diner's specific allergies, with kitchen-verified data. Target market: the hundreds of millions with food allergies or intolerances and the restaurants serving them.
features: Travel mode with local allergen-term translation
Order-ahead notes routed to the kitchen

impact.social: Restores safe, confident dining out for an underserved at-risk group.
impact.economic: Captures spend allergic diners currently avoid; restaurants reach loyal customers.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances verified allergen data and personalized food-safety filtering.

projects.name: ParkPool
projects.shortDescription: Cities choke on cars circling for parking while countless private driveways and lots sit empty. ParkPool is a marketplace that lets owners rent out unused parking by the hour or month to nearby drivers. Target market: urban drivers seeking parking and owners of underused private spaces.
features: List and book private parking spaces by hour or month
Dynamic pricing by demand and location
Access control via code or guided entry
Integration with navigation for guided arrival

impact.social: Reduces parking stress and congestion in dense neighborhoods.
impact.economic: Owners earn passive income; drivers save on commercial parking.
impact.environmental: Less circling for parking cuts urban emissions.
impact.technological: Advances peer-to-peer spatial-asset marketplaces.

projects.name: TuneUp
projects.shortDescription: Aspiring musicians quit because lessons are expensive, infrequent, and disconnected from daily practice. TuneUp pairs affordable online lessons with practice tracking and feedback between sessions to keep learners progressing. Target market: hobbyist music learners and independent music teachers.
features: Online lesson marketplace matched by instrument and level
Practice tracking with goals between lessons
Audio feedback on tempo, pitch, and timing

impact.social: Sustains music education and supports independent teacher livelihoods.
impact.economic: Lower-cost lessons widen access; teachers gain flexible income.
impact.environmental: Remote lessons cut commuting.
impact.technological: Advances music-practice analytics and feedback.

projects.name: MicroLease
projects.shortDescription: Aspiring micro-entrepreneurs can't afford the productive assets—sewing machines, tools, equipment—they need to earn a living. MicroLease offers affordable rent-to-own financing for income-generating assets with payments tied to earnings. Target market: micro-entrepreneurs and informal-sector workers in emerging markets.
features: Alternative credit scoring from repayment history
Graduation path to ownership and larger assets

impact.social: Unlocks self-employment for people the formal system excludes.
impact.economic: Productive assets can multiply a micro-entrepreneur's income substantially.
impact.environmental: Shared/recovered assets extend equipment lifecycle.
impact.technological: Advances earnings-linked financing and alternative scoring.

projects.name: WasteWise
projects.shortDescription: Recycling rates stay low because residents are confused about what goes where and pickup is inconsistent. WasteWise gives households clear, local recycling guidance and coordinates pickup of recyclables and hard-to-dispose items. Target market: households, municipalities, and recyclers.
features: Local, item-specific recycling guidance via photo or search
Scheduled pickup matching for recyclables and bulky items
Household recycling streak and impact tracking

impact.social: Empowers residents to recycle correctly and reduce confusion.
impact.economic: Reduces contamination costs and recovers valuable materials.
impact.environmental: Higher correct-recycling rates divert significant waste from landfill.
impact.technological: Advances localized waste classification and collection logistics.

projects.name: SeniorRide
projects.shortDescription: Elderly and mobility-limited people miss medical appointments because standard rideshare can't meet their accessibility and assistance needs. SeniorRide provides booked, accessible, door-through-door non-emergency medical transport with trained drivers. Target market: seniors, caregivers, clinics, and payers covering transport.
features: Door-through-door assistance, not just curb drop-off
Caregiver and clinic booking on the rider's behalf
Real-time tracking for family and clinics

impact.social: Keeps vulnerable people connected to care and community.
impact.economic: Reduces costly missed-appointment and emergency-transport spend.
impact.environmental: Coordinated recurring trips improve vehicle efficiency.
impact.technological: Advances accessible, assistance-aware transport dispatch.

projects.name: ScriptSaver
projects.shortDescription: Patients overpay for prescriptions because drug prices vary wildly between pharmacies and discount programs they never see. ScriptSaver compares real prescription prices across pharmacies and surfaces the best coupons, generics, and assistance programs. Target market: uninsured and underinsured patients and cost-conscious consumers.
features: Real-time prescription price comparison across pharmacies
Price alerts when a cheaper option appears

impact.social: Reduces medication non-adherence driven by cost.
impact.economic: Patients can save 30-80% on common prescriptions by switching options.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances real-time drug-price transparency.

projects.name: NestEgg
projects.shortDescription: Informal and gig workers have no pension and face poverty in old age because retirement products assume formal employment. NestEgg is a flexible micro-pension that lets informal workers save tiny amounts toward retirement with optional matching. Target market: informal-sector and gig workers in emerging markets.
features: Withdrawal rules tuned to protect retirement savings

impact.social: Addresses old-age poverty for workers the formal system ignores.
impact.economic: Even small consistent contributions compound into meaningful retirement buffers.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances inclusive micro-pension infrastructure.

projects.name: PlotShare
projects.shortDescription: Aspiring urban growers can't find land while vacant lots and underused yards sit idle in cities. PlotShare matches would-be gardeners to available plots, allotments, and shareable yards, with tools and guidance. Target market: urban residents, landowners with idle space, and city greening programs.
features: Plot and shareable-yard listing and matching
Growing guidance tailored to plot and climate

impact.social: Builds food access, green space, and community in cities.
impact.economic: Grows household food value and activates idle land.
impact.environmental: Urban growing improves greening, biodiversity, and local food miles.
impact.technological: Advances land-matching and urban-agriculture coordination.

projects.name: StudyPods
projects.shortDescription: Students learn better together but struggle to find reliable, motivated study partners and stay accountable. StudyPods forms small peer study groups by course and goals, with shared schedules and accountability tracking. Target market: university and exam-prep students.
features: Peer study-group matching by course and goals
Shared study schedules and session reminders
Resource and note sharing within pods
Optional tutor escalation for stuck topics

impact.social: Reduces isolation and improves learning through peer support.
impact.economic: Free/low-cost peer support supplements expensive tutoring.
impact.environmental: Remote-capable; reduces commuting to study spaces.
impact.technological: Advances peer-matching and accountability systems.

projects.name: ChoreChain
projects.shortDescription: Parents struggle to teach kids responsibility and money sense without nagging or losing track of allowances. ChoreChain turns household chores into a clear system of tasks, verification, and earned allowance that builds financial habits. Target market: families with school-age children.
features: Photo or parent verification of completed tasks

impact.social: Builds responsibility and money habits in children early.
impact.economic: Teaches saving behaviors with lifelong financial benefit.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances gamified family financial education.

projects.name: LocalLens
projects.shortDescription: Small neighborhood service businesses are nearly invisible online while residents struggle to find and book them. LocalLens is a hyperlocal directory and booking layer that surfaces nearby services with real availability and reviews. Target market: local service businesses and neighborhood residents.
features: Hyperlocal service discovery by proximity and need
Verified local reviews tied to bookings

impact.social: Strengthens local economies and neighborhood connection.
impact.economic: Captures demand that leaks to distant platforms for small providers.
impact.environmental: Promotes nearby services with shorter travel.
impact.technological: Advances hyperlocal discovery and booking.

projects.name: ReturnPal
projects.shortDescription: Returning online purchases means inconvenient trips to carrier offices, so people keep unwanted items or returns pile up. ReturnPal is a peer-to-peer network of nearby drop-off hosts who accept and consolidate returns for a small fee. Target market: online shoppers, retailers, and people seeking flexible side income.

impact.social: Removes friction from returns and creates flexible local income.
impact.economic: Reduces retailer return-processing cost and shopper hassle.
impact.environmental: Consolidated returns reduce individual return trips.
impact.technological: Advances peer-to-peer reverse-logistics networks.

projects.name: MindBridge
projects.shortDescription: People seeking mental health care face long waits, confusing options, and uncertainty about cost and fit. MindBridge matches individuals to licensed therapists by need, specialty, insurance, and availability, and streamlines booking. Target market: people seeking mental health support and licensed mental health providers.
features: Therapist matching by need, specialty, and approach
Fast booking with reduced wait times

regulatory: Mental health licensing and scope rules

impact.social: Reduces barriers to timely, appropriate mental health care.
impact.economic: Faster matching cuts costly delays and improves provider utilization.
impact.environmental: Teletherapy reduces travel.
impact.technological: Advances care matching and access in mental health.

projects.name: CrowdRepair
projects.shortDescription: Neighborhoods see broken benches, unlit paths, and neglected public spaces that no agency fixes quickly. CrowdRepair lets residents propose, fund, and coordinate small civic improvements with transparent budgets and local matching. Target market: residents, neighborhood associations, and municipalities.
features: Resident proposals for local improvement micro-projects

impact.social: Empowers communities to improve shared spaces and build cohesion.
impact.economic: Mobilizes small local funds and matching for high-ROI civic fixes.
impact.environmental: Supports greening and walkable, maintained public space.
impact.technological: Advances civic crowdfunding and project coordination.

projects.name: SwapStyle
projects.shortDescription: People wear a fraction of their wardrobe and constantly buy new clothes, driving cost and textile waste. SwapStyle is a clothing-swap subscription that lets members rotate quality garments with others instead of buying new. Target market: fashion-conscious, sustainability-minded consumers.

impact.social: Makes refreshing a wardrobe affordable and sustainable.
impact.economic: Cuts clothing spend through rotation instead of purchase.
impact.environmental: Each swap displaces new-garment production and textile waste.
impact.technological: Advances circular-fashion logistics and matching.

projects.name: PantryPlan
projects.shortDescription: Households waste a third of the food they buy through poor planning and forgotten ingredients. PantryPlan plans meals around what's already at home, generates efficient grocery lists, and reduces waste and spend. Target market: budget- and waste-conscious households.
features: Meal planning around existing pantry inventory

impact.social: Helps families eat well on a budget with less stress.
impact.economic: Cutting household food waste can save families hundreds per year.
impact.environmental: Reducing food waste avoids significant embedded water and emissions.
impact.technological: Advances pantry-aware meal planning and optimization.

projects.name: ColdProof
projects.shortDescription: Up to 25% of vaccines are wasted globally because cold-chain breaches go undetected until doses are already spoiled. ColdProof is a reusable, $4 temperature-logging tag plus a phone-readable dashboard that gives an unambiguous go/no-go verdict for each vaccine carton at the point of use. The target market is last-mile health systems, NGOs, and pharmaceutical distributors operating in low-infrastructure regions.
features: Reusable NFC temperature tag readable by any phone with no app install required for a basic verdict
Cumulative thermal-exposure model that reflects actual vaccine stability curves, not just threshold breaches
Tamper-evident commissioning so a tag cannot be reset to hide a breach
Chain-of-custody log capturing every scan, location, and handler along the route
Predictive failure alerts when a fridge or carrier trends toward breach before spoilage occurs
Multi-product profiles so one tag serves vaccines, insulin, and biologics with different tolerances
Offline ledger that syncs verdicts when connectivity returns
Open verification API for ministries of health and auditors

operational: Tag manufacturing and calibration supply chain

impact.social: Increases trust in vaccine programs and protects vulnerable populations from receiving inert doses.
impact.economic: Reducing 25% vaccine wastage toward 5% can save large programs millions of dollars annually in product and re-vaccination costs.
impact.environmental: Reusable tags and reduced spoilage cut medical waste and the carbon cost of re-shipping doses.
impact.technological: Advances low-cost, low-power sensing and verifiable chain-of-custody for temperature-sensitive goods broadly.

projects.name: GridSink
projects.shortDescription: As home batteries, EVs, and heat pumps proliferate, grids face dangerous demand peaks while distributed storage sits idle. GridSink aggregates thousands of these home assets into a coordinated virtual power plant that balances the grid and pays owners for flexibility. The target market is utilities, grid operators, and prosumers in regions with high renewable penetration.
features: Universal device-abstraction layer that controls batteries, EV chargers, and heat pumps across vendor brands
Real-time dispatch engine that responds to grid signals within sub-second to multi-minute windows
Owner comfort guarantees so home needs (charged car by 7am, warm house) are never violated
Transparent earnings dashboard showing exactly what each dispatch event paid the owner
Forecasting of household load and solar to bid flexibility into energy markets
Grid-operator interface compliant with market settlement and telemetry standards
Islanding/backup mode prioritizing the home during outages over market participation
Carbon-aware scheduling that shifts loads to the greenest grid hours

regulatory: Grid interconnection and DER aggregation rules

impact.social: Improves grid resilience and keeps the lights on during stress events for whole communities.
impact.economic: Owners can earn $200-1000+ per year per asset; utilities defer costly peaker plants and grid upgrades.
impact.environmental: Shifting load to renewable hours and avoiding fossil peakers can cut grid carbon intensity by measurable percentages.
impact.technological: Advances real-time distributed control, edge orchestration, and grid-interactive standards.

projects.name: PhageMatch
projects.shortDescription: Antibiotic-resistant infections kill over a million people a year, and bacteriophage therapy—using viruses that target specific bacteria—remains slow and ad hoc to deploy. PhageMatch is a clinical service and registry that rapidly matches a patient's resistant bacterial isolate to characterized therapeutic phages and coordinates compassionate-use logistics. The target market is hospitals, infectious-disease clinicians, and phage research centers.
features: Searchable registry of characterized therapeutic phages with host-range and safety metadata
Rapid in-silico matching of a patient isolate's genome to candidate phages
Susceptibility-testing workflow coordination between hospital labs and phage banks
Regulatory-pathway assistant for compassionate-use and expanded-access filings
Cold-chain and chain-of-custody tracking for phage shipment to bedside
Outcome registry feeding real-world evidence back into matching models
Phage cocktail designer to pre-empt resistance to single phages
Secure clinician collaboration and case-consult workspace

impact.social: Offers a last-line option for patients with untreatable infections, addressing a top global health threat.
impact.economic: Shorter ICU stays and avoided treatment failures can save tens of thousands of dollars per severe case.
impact.environmental: Targeted phage therapy avoids broad-spectrum antibiotic overuse that drives resistance.
impact.technological: Advances genomic matching, phage characterization standards, and real-world evidence infrastructure.

projects.name: FitTwin
projects.shortDescription: Apparel e-commerce suffers 25-40% return rates driven mostly by sizing mismatches, crushing margins and generating enormous waste. FitTwin builds an accurate body model from a quick phone scan and predicts fit and drape for each specific garment, cutting size-related returns. The target market is online apparel retailers and the shoppers frustrated by inconsistent sizing.
features: Privacy-preserving body measurement from a two-photo phone scan, processed on-device
Garment-specific fit prediction using each brand's true measurements, not generic size charts
Drape and stretch modeling so fit reflects fabric behavior, not just dimensions
Confidence-scored size recommendation with fit-intent options (snug/relaxed)
Retailer analytics on which SKUs cause the most fit-driven returns
Reusable body profile portable across participating retailers with user consent
Inclusive sizing coverage validated across diverse body types
Returns-reduction reporting tied directly to sustainability and margin metrics

operational: Bias and accuracy validation across body types

impact.social: Improves shopping confidence and inclusivity for shoppers poorly served by standard sizing.
impact.economic: Cutting return rates from ~30% toward ~15% can save retailers millions annually in reverse-logistics cost.
impact.environmental: Returns generate significant CO2 and landfill waste; halving fit-returns meaningfully cuts both.
impact.technological: Advances on-device 3D body estimation and physically-based garment simulation at scale.

projects.name: FloodMesh
projects.shortDescription: Urban flash floods kill and displace people in minutes, yet most vulnerable neighborhoods have no localized early warning. FloodMesh is a low-cost community sensor network plus predictive model that issues hyperlocal flood alerts and evacuation guidance street by street. The target market is municipal disaster agencies and flood-prone urban communities, especially in monsoon and coastal regions.
features: Low-cost ultrasonic water-level sensors deployable on drains, canals, and underpasses
Mesh networking so sensors relay data even when cellular networks fail
Street-level flood prediction fusing sensor data, rainfall radar, and terrain models
Multilingual, multi-channel alerts via SMS, app, sirens, and loudspeaker integration
Community-maintainable hardware with simple field repair and solar power
Evacuation routing that steers people away from flooding roads in real time
Open data feed for emergency services and researchers
Post-event analytics to prioritize drainage and infrastructure investment

impact.social: Minutes of advance warning can save lives and reduce displacement in the most vulnerable neighborhoods.
impact.economic: Reduces flood damage costs and post-disaster recovery spending; targeted drainage investment improves ROI.
impact.environmental: Supports climate adaptation and informs nature-based drainage solutions.
impact.technological: Advances resilient mesh sensing and hyperlocal hydrological prediction.

projects.name: RetrofitROI
projects.shortDescription: Buildings drive nearly 40% of global emissions, yet owners stall on decarbonization retrofits because the cost, sequencing, and financing are opaque. RetrofitROI generates a prioritized, costed retrofit roadmap for any building and matches it to available incentives and financing. The target market is commercial and residential property owners, facility managers, and ESG-driven asset holders.
features: Building energy model built from utility bills, basic inputs, and optional sensor data
Prioritized retrofit roadmap ranked by carbon-per-dollar and payback period
Live incentive and rebate matching across local, regional, and national programs
Financing marketplace connecting projects to green loans and on-bill financing
Measurement & verification to prove realized savings post-retrofit
Portfolio view for owners managing many buildings with capital-planning tools
Contractor matching with verified retrofit specialists
Regulatory-compliance tracker for building performance standards and disclosure laws

regulatory: Financial product compliance for financing arm

impact.social: Improves indoor comfort, air quality, and energy affordability for occupants.
impact.economic: Identifies retrofits with 3-10 year paybacks and unlocks incentives owners routinely leave unclaimed.
impact.environmental: Targets the building sector's ~40% emissions share with measurable, verified carbon reductions per project.
impact.technological: Advances automated building energy modeling and incentive-matching at scale.

projects.name: SourceTrace
projects.shortDescription: Enterprises in regulated fields can't safely use AI outputs they cannot verify, because hallucinated or unsourced claims create legal and compliance risk. SourceTrace is a verification layer that grounds, traces, and audits every claim in an AI response back to authoritative sources before it reaches a regulated user. The target market is finance, healthcare, legal, and government AI deployments.
features: Claim-level provenance that links each generated assertion to a specific source passage
Real-time verification that flags unsupported or contradicted claims before display
Tamper-evident audit log of every AI output and its supporting evidence
Source-authority scoring so claims are graded by the trustworthiness of their basis
Configurable compliance policies per industry (e.g. cite-or-suppress for medical advice)
Human-review routing for high-stakes or low-confidence outputs
Model-agnostic integration that wraps any underlying LLM
Regulator-ready reporting exports for audits and investigations

regulatory: Sector-specific AI governance (e.g. EU AI Act tiers)

impact.social: Makes AI assistance trustworthy in high-stakes settings where errors harm people.
impact.economic: Reduces compliance risk and rework; enables AI adoption that was previously legally blocked.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances retrieval grounding, claim verification, and auditable AI infrastructure.

projects.name: CurbFlow
projects.shortDescription: City curbs are chaotically shared by deliveries, rideshare, parking, and buses, causing congestion, double-parking, and lost revenue. CurbFlow is a dynamic curb-management platform that prices and allocates curb space in real time and lets vehicles reserve loading slots. The target market is city transportation departments, delivery fleets, and mobility operators.
features: Digital curb inventory mapping every meter of curb and its current rules
Real-time, reservable loading slots for delivery and rideshare vehicles
Dynamic, demand-based pricing to balance turnover and reduce double-parking
Fleet integration so delivery vehicles route to guaranteed available curb space
Enforcement support via sensors and license-plate verification
Revenue and utilization analytics for city planners
Emissions tracking from reduced circling and idling
Public transparency portal showing curb policy and availability

impact.social: Reduces dangerous double-parking and frees curb space for accessibility and transit.
impact.economic: Increases curb revenue and cuts delivery dwell time and fleet costs.
impact.environmental: Less circling for parking cuts urban emissions; studies attribute ~30% of urban congestion to parking search.
impact.technological: Advances dynamic spatial resource allocation and fleet-city coordination.

projects.name: ScreenEar
projects.shortDescription: Undetected hearing loss in childhood derails language development and education, yet most low-resource schools have no screening access. ScreenEar turns a low-cost headset and a phone into a validated hearing-screening tool that non-specialists can administer to entire classrooms. The target market is school health programs, NGOs, and pediatric services in low-resource regions.
features: Validated pure-tone screening protocol runnable by trained non-specialists
Calibrated low-cost headset paired with an ambient-noise check for reliable results
Automated pass/refer decision with referral routing to clinics
Batch classroom workflow to screen many children efficiently
Offline operation with later sync to health records
Population dashboards for health programs to target interventions
Follow-up tracking so referred children actually reach care

impact.social: Early detection prevents lifelong educational and developmental disadvantage for affected children.
impact.economic: Early intervention is far cheaper than lifelong support; improves educational and earning outcomes.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint; reusable hardware.
impact.technological: Advances validated low-cost clinical screening on consumer hardware.

projects.name: MedReturn
projects.shortDescription: Vast quantities of unused, in-date medication are destroyed while free clinics face shortages, a coordination failure with legal and logistical roots. MedReturn is a compliant redistribution platform that safely matches surplus sealed medication from verified sources to licensed clinics serving the uninsured. The target market is charitable pharmacies, free clinics, and jurisdictions with drug-repository laws.
features: Eligibility engine encoding each jurisdiction's drug-donation and repository laws
Verified-source intake limited to sealed, in-date, properly stored medication
Real-time matching of surplus inventory to clinic needs
Chain-of-custody and storage-condition tracking for compliance
Recall and expiry monitoring to pull affected stock automatically
Pharmacist verification workflow before any dispensing
Impact reporting on value redistributed and patients served
Audit-ready records for regulators and donors

impact.social: Improves medication access for uninsured and low-income patients who currently go without.
impact.economic: Redirects millions of dollars of otherwise-destroyed medication to people who need it.
impact.environmental: Reduces pharmaceutical incineration waste and its environmental burden.
impact.technological: Advances compliance-encoded matching for highly regulated goods.

projects.name: SpendLens
projects.shortDescription: Mid-market finance teams lose money to duplicate invoices, billing errors, and maverick spend hidden in transaction noise. SpendLens continuously audits AP/AR data to flag anomalies, duplicates, and policy violations before payment. Target market: mid-market finance and procurement departments.
features: Anomaly flagging on amounts, vendors, and timing
Forecast of cash impact from flagged issues

impact.social: Frees finance staff from tedious reconciliation to higher-value work.
impact.economic: Recovering even 0.5-1% of spend leakage is significant at mid-market scale.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint; reduces paper invoicing.
impact.technological: Advances anomaly detection over financial transaction graphs.

projects.name: ChurnRadar
projects.shortDescription: B2B subscription businesses discover churn only after a customer leaves, when it's too late to intervene. ChurnRadar predicts account churn from product, support, and engagement signals and triggers tailored retention playbooks. Target market: B2B SaaS revenue and customer-success teams.
features: Account-level churn-risk scoring from multi-source signals
Early-warning triggers ahead of renewal windows
Root-cause attribution for each at-risk account
CS workload prioritization by risk and value
Integrations with CRM, product analytics, and support

impact.social: Improves customer outcomes through proactive support.
impact.economic: Reducing churn a few points compounds materially on recurring revenue.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
impact.technological: Advances multi-signal predictive retention modeling.

projects.name: ContractClock
projects.shortDescription: Companies miss renewal deadlines, auto-renewals, and obligations buried in scattered contracts, causing surprise costs and risk. ContractClock extracts key dates and obligations from a contract repository and tracks them to deadline. Target market: legal, procurement, and finance teams managing many agreements.
features: Automated extraction of dates, obligations, and clauses
Spend and commitment roll-up by counterparty

impact.social: Reduces stressful fire-drills from missed obligations.
impact.economic: Avoids surprise auto-renewals and penalties; surfaces renegotiation leverage.
impact.environmental: Reduces paper contract handling.
impact.technological: Advances obligation-aware contract intelligence.

projects.name: VendorVet
projects.shortDescription: Organizations carry hidden risk from third-party vendors whose security and compliance posture changes silently after onboarding. VendorVet continuously monitors supplier risk—security, financial, and compliance—and alerts on deterioration. Target market: enterprise risk, procurement, and security teams.
features: Continuous third-party risk scoring across security and compliance
Automated security questionnaire collection and validation
Breach and incident monitoring per vendor
Concentration-risk mapping across the supplier base
Tiered alerting by criticality of the vendor

impact.social: Protects customers and partners from supply-chain breaches.
impact.economic: Avoids costly third-party breach and compliance-failure events.
impact.environmental: Negligible direct footprint.
